notes_md:
**kêu** = to call out to someone, to shout, or to make a sound. It’s used when you get someone’s attention by voice.

You use it for calling a person, calling someone to do something, or calling someone by name.  

It also means “to cry/make a sound” for babies and animals. Another very common meaning is “to order” (food/drinks) and “to complain” (often in **kêu ca**).

Close cousin: **gọi** (also “call”), but *kêu* feels more like calling out loudly or audibly.

content.sentence: Con chó ___ khi có người lạ.

translation.en: The dog barks when there is a stranger.

definitions.vi: Phát ra âm thanh hoặc tiếng kêu lớn.
definitions.es: Hacer un sonido o grito fuerte.
definitions.en: To make a loud sound or cry.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I will call you if I need help with this homework.
Every morning, mom usually calls the children to get up for school.
I forgot to call the waiter to bring more water to our table.
exampleSentences: Em sẽ kêu anh nếu em cần giúp đỡ với bài tập này.
Mỗi sáng mẹ thường kêu các con dậy đi học.
Anh quên kêu phục vụ mang thêm nước cho bàn mình.
